title: BGP Session Drop
symptoms: BGP neighbor state transitions to Idle or Active
Prefix count drops to 0 for the affected peer
Traffic re-routes or black-holes depending on redundancy
steps: Check BGP summary: 'show bgp summary' — identify which neighbor is down and how long
Verify TCP reachability to the peer: 'telnet <peer-IP> 179' — if it fails, it's a routing/firewall issue
Check interface toward the peer: 'show interfaces <intf>' for errors or line-protocol down
Review BGP notification message: 'show bgp neighbors <peer> | inc notification' — identifies exact teardown reason
Verify BGP password if MD5 is used: 'show bgp neighbors <peer> | inc password'
Check if peer's AS number or update-source changed in the config
Look for route-policy or prefix-list blocking updates: 'show bgp neighbors <peer> advertised-routes'
Verify hold-timer expiry: if hold timer expired, check for CPU spikes or packet loss on the link
resolution: If TCP fails: fix routing to the peer or remove firewall block on port 179. If hold timer expired: investigate link quality or increase hold-time. If notification is 'Cease': peer administratively shut — coordinate with the ISP or remote NOC. Use 'clear ip bgp <peer> soft' for soft reset without dropping session.
escalation: If the peer is a Tier-1 transit provider or IX peer, notify the ISP NOC immediately. If more than 50% of prefixes are lost, escalate to Network Architect within 15 minutes.
reference: Emircom BGP Peer Management SOP — Rev 3.1
